zh.lifeSummary: 當Miss de Beatrice De St Martin於1150年出生在英國素可時，她的父親Jordon St. Martin-34歲，母親Agnes Grener34歲。她於大約1170年與Hugh De Lundresford在英國英格蘭素可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Miss de Beatrice De St Martin于1150年出生在英国素可时，她的父亲Jordon St. Martin-34岁，母亲Agnes Grener34岁。她于大约1170年与Hugh De Lundresford在英国英格兰素可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  French: habitational name from any of the places so called, for example in Normandy, named from a religious dedication to a (male) St. Clair.
2  North American variant spelling of   St  .   Claire  .

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Miss de Beatrice De St Martin was born in 1150, in Sussex, England, her father, Jordon St. Martin, was -34 and her mother, Agnes Grener, was 34. She married Hugh De Lundresford about 1170, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Miss de Beatrice De St Martin est née en 1150, à Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Jordon St. Martin, avait -34 ans et sa mère, Agnes Grener, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh De Lundresford environ 1170, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Miss de Beatrice De St Martin 1150 in Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jordon St. Martin -34 und ihre Mutter Agnes Grener 34. Sie hat um 1170 in Sussex, England, United Kingdom Hugh De Lundresford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Miss de Beatrice De St Martin è nata il nel 1150 nel Sussex, England, sua padre, Jordon St. Martin, aveva -34 anni e sua madre, Agnes Grener, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Hugh De Lundresford circa 1170, nel Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli.

ko.lifeSummary: Miss de Beatrice De St Martin(이)가 1150년 영국 수세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jordon St. Martin(은)는 -34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Grener(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1170년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 Hugh De Lundresford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Miss de Beatrice De St Martin nasceu em 1150, em Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Jordon St. Martin, tinha -34 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Grener, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Hugh De Lundresford aproximadamente 1170, em Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Miss de Beatrice De St Martin родилась в 1150 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Jordon St. Martin было -34 год, а матери, которую звали Agnes Grener, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh De Lundresford около 1170 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Miss de Beatrice De St Martin nació en 1150, en Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Jordon St. Martin, tenía -34 años y su mamá, Agnes Grener, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Hugh De Lundresford aproximadamente 1170, en Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos.
